gold_standard: Patient is a 31-year-old female with a confirmed diagnosis of moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis (ICD-10: L20.9) with objective disease burden documented at 35% BSA involvement and an EASI score of 28.4, consistent with severe disease classification. Dermatology supports initiation of dupilumab 300mg SC every two weeks based on guideline-concordant progression through step therapy. Authorization is medically necessary to achieve disease control and prevent long-term sequelae including infection and scarring.

gold_standard: Following review of the submitted clinical documentation for lumbar spine MRI (CPT 72148), this request does not meet Cigna medical necessity criteria at this time. Current clinical guidelines and Cigna coverage policy require a minimum 4-6 week trial of conservative management — including physical therapy and pharmacologic therapy — prior to advanced imaging for non-specific low back pain in the absence of red flag symptoms. The submitted documentation does not demonstrate radicular symptoms, neurologic deficits, bowel or bladder dysfunction, fever, unexplained weight loss, or history of malignancy that would warrant expedited imaging. We recommend initiating a structured physical therapy program and trialing NSAIDs or acetaminophen. Should symptoms persist beyond 6 weeks or neurologic symptoms develop, resubmission with updated clinical documentation will be reviewed.

gold_standard: Following clinical review, the request for hereditary cancer syndrome gene panel testing (CPT 81408) does not meet Oscar Health medical necessity criteria. Coverage for comprehensive hereditary cancer panels requires documentation of personal history of cancer consistent with hereditary syndrome, or first- or second-degree family history meeting NCCN hereditary cancer testing guidelines (e.g., breast cancer <50, ovarian cancer at any age, two or more relatives with same cancer). The submitted documentation reflects neither a qualifying personal history nor a family history meeting these thresholds. We recommend the patient complete a genetic counseling consultation, after which the counselor can assess whether criteria are met and resubmit with appropriate clinical justification.

gold_standard: Following review, the request for dupilumab for severe asthma (J45.51) does not meet Aetna criteria at this time. Dupilumab as add-on maintenance therapy for asthma requires documentation of: (1) inadequate response to medium-to-high dose ICS/LABA combination therapy for a minimum of 3 months, (2) baseline FEV1 measurement, and (3) eosinophil count or FeNO level to establish type 2 inflammatory phenotype eligibility. The submitted record reflects no prior ICS or ICS/LABA therapy, and required objective measurements are absent. We recommend initiating guideline-directed step-up therapy beginning with ICS, followed by ICS/LABA if needed, with pulmonology consultation to evaluate for biologic candidacy after an adequate treatment trial.

gold_standard: Following review, transcranial magnetic stimulation (CPT 90867) does not meet Cigna coverage criteria for major depressive disorder at this time. Cigna requires documentation of inadequate response to at least two adequate antidepressant trials (adequate defined as therapeutic dose for minimum 6-8 weeks) prior to TMS authorization. The submitted record reflects a single antidepressant trial at 6 weeks, which has not yet reached the minimum adequate duration for response assessment. We recommend completing the current sertraline trial to 8 weeks and trialing at least one additional agent or augmentation strategy. Psychiatry referral may also assist in optimizing the pharmacotherapy regimen before TMS is considered.

gold_standard: This 28-year-old male carries a confirmed diagnosis of paroxysmal nocturnal hemoglobinuria (D59.5) by flow cytometry with clone size of 72% and LDH 4.8x ULN indicating active complement-mediated hemolysis. Two thrombotic events in 18 months document high-risk disease phenotype. Complement inhibitor therapy is the standard of care for symptomatic PNH and does not require prior therapy failure — it is first-line per international PNH guidelines. Meningococcal vaccination has been completed as required prior to eculizumab initiation. Tertiary hematology supports therapy. Authorization of eculizumab is medically necessary to reduce thrombotic risk and hemolysis in this high-risk PNH patient.

gold_standard: Following review, repeat screening mammography (CPT 77067) does not meet Anthem medical necessity criteria at this time. The patient's most recent mammogram 8 months ago returned BI-RADS 1 (negative) with no findings warranting shortened surveillance interval. Anthem coverage guidelines support annual screening mammography for average-risk women ages 40 and older. A shortened interval before 12 months requires documentation of: prior BI-RADS 3 or higher finding, high-risk designation (BRCA mutation, lifetime risk >20%, prior chest radiation), or a new clinical finding such as a palpable mass. None of these indications are present. Annual screening is recommended beginning 4 months from now.

gold_standard: This 61-year-old female presents with seropositive RA (M05.79) with high disease activity (DAS28 5.9, RF 210 IU/mL, anti-CCP 380 U/mL). Three prior treatment lines have been completed: methotrexate 25mg weekly for 8 months (inadequate response), leflunomide 20mg for 6 months (inadequate response), and adalimumab 40mg q2w for 12 months (primary non-response). TNF primary non-response is a clinically recognized indication for switching to a biologic with a distinct mechanism of action. Rheumatology recommends abatacept based on evidence supporting its use in TNF-IR patients. Infectious disease screening is negative. Authorization is medically necessary and meets BCBS FEP biologic step therapy criteria.

gold_standard: This 39-year-old male requests naltrexone/bupropion (Contrave) for obesity (E66.09, BMI 37.8) with a history of alcohol use disorder in remission >12 months (F10.20). Psychiatry confirms sustained remission and current naltrexone use for AUD maintenance, supporting the naltrexone component. However, the bupropion component warrants clinical review in the context of AUD history given seizure risk considerations, despite no documented seizure history and normal LFTs. Additionally, Cigna criteria for weight management pharmacotherapy typically require documentation of prior lifestyle intervention. This case is routed for clinical pharmacist review to evaluate the risk-benefit of bupropion in this specific psychiatric context and confirm lifestyle intervention documentation.
